name: Gentle Exercise
description: Simple seated exercises for gentle movement.
technical_summary: Provides guided seated exercise instructions from a curated list with steps and benefit descriptions.

exercises.name: Seated Arm Raise
exercises.steps: Sit comfortably. Slowly raise both arms above your head. Hold for 5 seconds. Lower slowly. Repeat 3 times.
exercises.benefit: Improves shoulder mobility and circulation.

exercises.name: Ankle Circles
exercises.steps: Sit in a chair with feet flat. Lift one foot and rotate your ankle in small circles â€” 5 times one way, then 5 times the other. Switch feet.
exercises.benefit: Keeps ankles flexible and helps prevent stiffness.

exercises.name: Gentle Neck Stretch
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Slowly tilt your head to the right, bringing your ear toward your shoulder. Hold for 10 seconds. Return to center. Repeat on the left side.
exercises.benefit: Relieves neck tension and improves range of motion.

exercises.name: Hand Squeeze
exercises.steps: Hold a soft ball or rolled-up sock. Squeeze gently for 5 seconds. Release. Repeat 10 times with each hand.
exercises.benefit: Strengthens grip and keeps fingers nimble.

exercises.name: Seated Marching
exercises.steps: Sit tall in a chair. Slowly lift one knee up, then lower it. Alternate legs as if marching in place. Do 10 on each side.
exercises.benefit: Gets your blood flowing and strengthens your legs.

exercises.name: Shoulder Rolls
exercises.steps: Sit up straight. Roll both shoulders forward in a slow circle 5 times. Then roll them backward 5 times.
exercises.benefit: Releases shoulder tension and improves posture.

exercises.name: Finger Stretches
exercises.steps: Hold one hand out flat. Spread your fingers wide and hold for 5 seconds. Then make a loose fist. Repeat 5 times on each hand.
exercises.benefit: Keeps hands and fingers flexible and reduces stiffness.

exercises.name: Seated Side Bend
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Place your right hand on your right thigh. Slowly reach your left arm up and lean gently to the right. Hold for 5 seconds. Repeat on the other side. Do 3 times each.
exercises.benefit: Stretches the sides of your body and improves flexibility.

exercises.name: Chin Tucks
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Gently pull your chin straight back, making a slight double chin. Hold for 5 seconds. Release. Repeat 5 times.
exercises.benefit: Strengthens neck muscles and improves posture.

exercises.name: Toe Taps
exercises.steps: Sit with both feet flat on the floor. Keeping your heels down, lift your toes up as high as comfortable. Lower them. Repeat 10 times.
exercises.benefit: Improves circulation in the lower legs and feet.

exercises.name: Wrist Rotations
exercises.steps: Extend your arms out in front of you. Slowly rotate your wrists in circles â€” 5 times one direction, then 5 times the other.
exercises.benefit: Keeps wrists mobile and can help with arthritis discomfort.

exercises.name: Seated Cat Stretch
exercises.steps: Sit at the edge of your chair. Place both hands on your knees. Slowly arch your back and look up. Then gently round your back and look down. Repeat 3 times.
exercises.benefit: Gently mobilizes the spine and relieves back tension.

exercises.name: Deep Breathing
exercises.steps: Sit comfortably. Breathe in slowly through your nose for 4 counts. Hold gently for 2 counts. Breathe out slowly through your mouth for 6 counts. Repeat 5 times.
exercises.benefit: Calms the mind, reduces stress, and improves oxygen flow.

exercises.name: Eye Circles
exercises.steps: Sit comfortably. Without moving your head, slowly move your eyes in a large circle â€” up, to the side, down, and back. Do 3 slow circles each direction.
exercises.benefit: Exercises eye muscles and can reduce eye strain.

exercises.name: Seated Heel Raises
exercises.steps: Sit with feet flat on the floor. Keeping your toes on the ground, lift both heels as high as comfortable. Lower slowly. Repeat 10 times.
exercises.benefit: Strengthens calf muscles and improves circulation.

exercises.name: Ear to Shoulder Stretch
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Drop your right ear slowly toward your right shoulder. Use your right hand to add very gentle pressure. Hold 10 seconds. Repeat on the left.
exercises.benefit: Releases tight neck and upper shoulder muscles.

exercises.name: Seated Torso Twist
exercises.steps: Sit tall with hands on your thighs. Slowly turn your upper body to the right, looking over your right shoulder. Hold 5 seconds. Return to center. Repeat on the left. Do 3 times each side.
exercises.benefit: Improves spinal rotation and digestion.

exercises.name: Thumb Touches
exercises.steps: Hold one hand out. Touch your thumb to each fingertip one at a time â€” index, middle, ring, little â€” then back again. Repeat 3 times on each hand.
exercises.benefit: Improves fine motor coordination and keeps fingers nimble.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Lean
exercises.steps: Sit at the edge of your chair. Place hands on your knees. Slowly lean forward from the hips until you feel a gentle stretch in your lower back. Hold 10 seconds. Sit back up slowly.
exercises.benefit: Gently stretches the lower back and hamstrings.

exercises.name: Lip Breathing
exercises.steps: Breathe in slowly through your nose for 2 counts. Then breathe out through pursed lips (as if blowing out a candle) for 4 counts. Repeat 6 times.
exercises.benefit: Slows breathing, improves lung efficiency, and is very calming.

exercises.name: Clapping Hands
exercises.steps: Hold your hands out in front of you. Clap slowly 10 times. Then clap with your left hand on top 10 times, and your right hand on top 10 times.
exercises.benefit: Stimulates circulation in the hands and is a gentle coordination exercise.

exercises.name: Seated Hip Circles
exercises.steps: Sit at the edge of your chair, feet flat. Place hands on your hips. Slowly make small circles with your hips â€” 5 times one way, 5 times the other.
exercises.benefit: Loosens the hip joints and lower back.

exercises.name: Eyebrow Raises
exercises.steps: Sit comfortably. Raise your eyebrows up as high as you can. Hold for 3 seconds. Let them relax. Repeat 5 times.
exercises.benefit: Exercises the muscles of the forehead and face.

exercises.name: Gentle Fist Pumps
exercises.steps: Make a loose fist with both hands. Slowly pump your arms up and down â€” not too high, just a gentle rhythm. Do 20 pumps.
exercises.benefit: Raises heart rate slightly and warms up the upper body.

exercises.name: Knee Lifts with Clap
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Lift your right knee. As it rises, clap your hands under it. Lower. Repeat on the left. Alternate for 10 total â€” 5 each side.
exercises.benefit: Combines arm and leg movement for gentle whole-body coordination.

exercises.name: Shoulder Squeeze
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Bring both shoulders back and squeeze your shoulder blades together. Hold for 5 seconds. Release. Repeat 8 times.
exercises.benefit: Strengthens upper back and counteracts rounded posture.

exercises.name: Head Turns
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Slowly turn your head to the right as far as comfortable. Hold 5 seconds. Return to center. Repeat on the left. Do 4 times each side.
exercises.benefit: Improves neck flexibility and reduces stiffness.

exercises.name: Gentle Leg Extension
exercises.steps: Sit tall. Slowly straighten your right leg out in front of you. Hold for 5 seconds. Lower slowly. Repeat with the left leg. Do 5 times each side.
exercises.benefit: Strengthens the thighs and keeps knees flexible.

exercises.name: Elbow Circles
exercises.steps: Raise both arms and touch your shoulders with your fingertips. Slowly make large circles with your elbows â€” forward 5 times, then backward 5 times.
exercises.benefit: Warms up the shoulders and upper arms.

exercises.name: Seated Star Stretch
exercises.steps: Sit at the edge of your chair. Spread your arms out wide and extend your legs slightly out to the sides. Take a deep breath in as you expand. Breathe out as you return to center. Repeat 4 times.
exercises.benefit: A gentle full-body stretch that feels refreshing and energizing.
